EUR: One-legged read: Policy (+1.2) is doing almost all the work; the other pillars are near neutral, so treat this as narrow, not broad-based.
FX and yields are decoupled — the real-yield reward/stress read is ambiguous this window; treat Pillar D lightly.
Carry crowded: carry-to-vol in the 87th percentile — a lot of carry riding on quiet vol, fragile to a vol spike.
JPY: Cheap and improving — the mean-reversion setup: valuation is below its own norm (-0.7) while fundamentals are turning up (+0.4).

Yield stress: real 10Y is high (z +1.7) but the currency weakens as yields rise (-0.21 corr) — risk-premium regime, not reward; real-yield support downweighted (mult -0.40). Note: cross vs USD partly confounds this in a strong-dollar tape.
GBP: Expensive and deteriorating — the vulnerable combination: rich valuation (+1.3) on a softening fundamental trajectory (-0.3).
Fat carry on a fragile base: out-yields the field in real terms (avg +1.2%) but sits on a stretched valuation — the classic carry-unwind setup.

Policy trap: inflation above target but growth soft and policy already tight (feasibility -1.5) — the hawkish tailwind is fading toward a stagflation drag; inflation's axis-1 contribution is dampened (mult -0.91).

Yield stress: real 10Y is high (z +1.6) but the currency weakens as yields rise (-0.36 corr) — risk-premium regime, not reward; real-yield support downweighted (mult -0.62). Note: cross vs USD partly confounds this in a strong-dollar tape.
